note:
E系列交易由PeopleFund牵头，包括里德·霍夫曼(Reid Hoffman)和马克·平卡斯(Mark Pincus)的Reinvent Capital、AmRest创始人亨利·麦戈文(Henry McGoven)、彭南特投资者(Pennant Investors)、VectoIQ合伙人史蒂夫·格斯基(Steve Girsky)、玛丽·陈(Mary Chan)和朱莉娅·斯泰恩(Julia Steyn。参与的先期投资者包括软银愿景基金(SoftBank Vision Fund)、门罗风险投资公司(Menlo Ventures)等。
这笔钱是在这家汽车共享服务在冠状病毒大流行之前和期间面临自己的一系列障碍之后获得的。据报道，今年1月，这家初创公司解雇了150名员工，减少了现场运营和众多全球团队的规模。据首席执行官萨姆·扎伊德(Sam Zaid)称，3月份的预订量下降了75%。Getround解雇了100名员工。扎伊德指出，部分原因是软银内部的挣扎，软银在2018年年中进行了3亿美元的D系列融资。
现在，扎伊德在给TechCrunch的一份声明中说，“软银在我们今年旅程的每个关键阶段，包括一月份和通过COVID，都是Getround的一个非常支持的合作伙伴。”如上所述，投资者参与了最新的融资。
这场大流行似乎已经从痛点变成了Getround的增长机会。在3月份的裁员之后，Getround在5月份看到了对其服务的需求回升：人们不想乘坐飞机，因为有抓到新冠肺炎的风险，但他们不介意开车。Getround专注于非接触式进入乘用车和改进平台。对于那些寻求旅行方式的人来说，到当地景点的短途旅行变得更容易实现，Getround发现了复苏的萌芽。据扎伊德称，到7月1日，Getround重新雇用了所有被暂时解雇的员工。
Zaid估计，Getround的全球收入比COVID之前的基线翻了一番多，并表示毛利率继续改善。这笔融资是在今年夏天筹集的，将用于帮助该业务投资于汽车技术，引入新的合作伙伴，并实现全球盈利。
受益于消费者新的旅行味蕾的并不只是Getround。Airbnb裁员1900人，占其全球员工总数的25%，该公司在专注于当地租赁业务方面找到了希望。据华尔街日报报道，今年6月，Airbnb完全重新设计了其网站和算法，向旅行者展示他们可以在附近的哪里租房。据传这家旅游公司将于11月上市。
在融资的同时，Getround还宣布了四名新的高管：北美业务主管丹·金，他曾担任Airbnb Plus的负责人和特斯拉的全球销售和交付主管；首席财务官劳拉·奥诺普琴科，他曾担任NerdWallet的首席财务官；负责人力和文化的副总裁蒂娅·戈登，曾担任谷歌的人力运营总监；以及客户体验副总裁露丝·扬库普，他曾担任OYO的客户体验副总裁。
note_en:
The Series E deal was led by PeopleFund with new investors including Reid Hoffman’s and Mark Pincus’ Reinvent Capital, AmRest founder Henry McGovern, Pennant Investors, VectoIQ partners Steve Girsky, Mary Chan, and Julia Steyn also deploying capital. Participating prior investors include SoftBank Vision Fund, Menlo Ventures, and more.
 The money comes after the car-sharing service faced its own set of hurdles before and during the coronavirus pandemic. In January, the startup reportedly laid off 150 employees, reducing field operations and the size of numerous global teams. In March, bookings dropped 75%, according to CEO Sam Zaid. Getaround laid off 100  employees. Zaid pointed to struggles within SoftBank, which did a $300 million Series D round in the company in mid-2018, as part of the reason.
  Now, Zaid says that “Softbank has been an extremely supportive partner to Getaround at every critical stage of our journey this year including in January and through COVID,” in a statement to TechCrunch. The investor, noted above, participated in the latest financing.
 The pandemic seems to have gone from a pain point to an opportunity for growth at Getaround. After the March layoffs, Getaround saw demand for its service come back in May: people didn’t want to fly because of the risk of catching COVID-19, but they didn’t mind driving. Getaround focused on contactless access to passenger cars and improving the platform. As short-distance travel to local joints became a more attainable option for those seeking a way to travel, Getaround found green shoots. By July 1, Getaround rehired all of its furloughed employees, according to Zaid.
 Zaid estimates that Getaround has seen worldwide revenue more than double from its pre-COVID baseline and says gross margins have continued to improve. The financing, which was raised in the summer, will be used to help the business invest in car technology, bring on new partners, and reach global profitability.
  Getaround isn’t alone in benefitting from consumers’ new travel tastebuds. Airbnb, which cut 1,900 jobs or  25% of its entire global workforce, is finding hope in focusing on local rentals. In June, according to  the WSJ, Airbnb entirely redesigned its website and algorithm to show travelers where they could rent in their neighborhoods. The travel company is rumored to be going public in November.
 Along with the financing, Getaround announced four new executives: Head of North American business  Dan Kim, who formerly worked as the head of Airbnb plus and head of global sales and delivery at Tesla; CFO  Laura Onopchenko, who is the former CFO of NerdWallet; vice president of people and culture Tia Gordon, formerly the director of people operations at Google; and vice president of customer experience Ruth Yankoupe, former vice president of Customer Experience at OYO.
title: 在短途旅行反弹的背景下，Getround筹集了1.4亿美元的E系列资金
title_en: Getaround raises a $140 million Series E amid rebound in short-distance travel

note:
首轮融资由Amiti Ventures牵头，F2风险投资公司、众筹平台OurCrowd、NextLeap Ventures和SeedIL Ventures参与其中。
Dataloop首席执行官Eran Shlomo说：“由于数据标签的限制和缺乏实时验证，许多组织继续努力将他们的AI和ML项目投入生产，而实时验证只能通过人工输入系统才能实现。”通过这项投资，我们与我们的合作伙伴致力于克服这些障碍，提供下一代数据管理工具，这些工具将改变人工智能行业，满足全球市场日益增长的创新需求。“。
在很大程度上，Dataloop专门帮助企业管理和注释他们的可视数据。它与客户所处的垂直领域无关，但我们谈论的是从机器人和无人机到零售和自动驾驶的任何东西。
该平台本身围绕“循环中的人”模型展开，该模型补充了自动化系统，使人类能够根据需要训练和修正模型。它将托管注释平台与Python SDK和面向开发人员的REST API相结合，以及在Kubernetes集群上运行以实现数据流自动化的无服务器功能即服务环境。
该公司成立于2017年。它将利用这笔新资金扩大其在美国和欧洲市场的影响力，这对以色列初创企业来说是相当标准的，并将扩大其工程团队。
note_en:
The Series A round was led by Amiti Ventures with participation from  F2 Venture Capital, crowdfunding platform  OurCrowd,  NextLeap Ventures and  SeedIL Ventures.
 “Many organizations continue to struggle with moving their AI and ML projects into production as a result of data labeling limitations and a lack of real time validation that can only be achieved with human input into the system,” said Dataloop CEO Eran Shlomo. “With this investment, we are committed, along with our partners, to overcoming these roadblocks and providing next generation data management tools that will transform the AI industry and meet the rising demand for innovation in global markets.”
   For the most part, Dataloop specializes in helping businesses manage and annotate their visual data. It’s agnostic to the vertical its customers are in, but we’re talking about anything from robotics and drones to  retail and autonomous driving.
 The platform itself centers around the ‘humans in the loop’ model that complements the automated systems with the ability for humans to train and correct the model as needed. It combines the hosted  annotation platform with a Python SDK and REST API for developers, as well as a serverless  Functions-as-a-Service environment that runs on top of a Kubernetes cluster for automating dataflows.
  The company was founded in 2017. It’ll use the new funding to grow its presence in the U.S. and European markets, something that’s pretty standard for Israeli startups, and build out its engineering team as well.
title: Dataloop为其AI数据管理平台筹集1100万美元的首轮融资
title_en: Dataloop raises $11M Series A round for its AI data management platform

该公司创始人艾米·范(Amy Fan)表示，为医疗补助或保险不足的女性提供的使命不应被剥夺，与收入更有保障或医疗保险更好的患者享有的护理相同。
这项使命和公司的技术已经成功地说服了一大批投资者，他们已经向这家新的初创公司投入了510万美元的种子资金。Third Prime主导了这轮融资，其中包括来自市政厅风险投资公司、钢铁天空风险投资公司、阿格拉风险投资公司、姜饼资本公司、Rucker Park资本公司、预测风险投资公司以及斯图·利比、佐伊·巴里和万·李柱等天使投资者的投资。
“接受医疗补助、保险不足或没有医疗保险的女性往往很难获得生殖健康服务，随着新冠肺炎大流行限制面对面预约，这种挣扎只会加剧，”Towyeight Health的联合创始人艾米·范(Amy Fan)在一份声明中表示。“我们正在争取医疗公平，确保所有女性，特别是BIPOC女性和来自低收入背景的女性，都能获得高质量、有尊严和方便的医疗服务。”
为了确保其服务于服务不足的社区，该公司与面向女性的劳动力进入计划Townless Closet以及纽约市立大学(City University Of New York)生态系统中的8所学院合作，其中包括拉瓜迪亚学院(LaGuardia College)，该学院有4.5万名学生，其中70%来自年收入低于3万美元的家庭。
该公司的服务目前覆盖佛罗里达州、马里兰州、纽约、新泽西州、北卡罗来纳州和宾夕法尼亚州，是唯一家接受医疗补助的专注于避孕服务的远程医疗公司。
这家公司有多棒的另一个例子是，它还致力于通过与贝德赛德的避孕通道基金合作，为无法自掏腰包且没有保险的女性提供免费节育服务。该公司还将2%的收入捐赠给Bedsider和国家生殖健康研究所。(各位，这家公司太棒了。)。
要注册这项服务，新客户需要在网上填写一份医疗问卷。一旦问卷在24小时内由美国委员会认证的医生审查，客户就可以获得100多个FDA批准的避孕药、贴片、戒指、针剂和紧急避孕药的品牌，并在3天内收到货件。
该公司表示，Twentyeight Health通过在线音频咨询和医生随访消息提供持续的护理，以讨论有关更新处方或解决副作用的问题。
Twentyeight Health的联合创始人布鲁诺·范·图伊科姆(Bruno Van Tuykom)在一份声明中说，“今天，低收入女性意外怀孕的可能性是美国普通女性的三倍，全国近三分之一的医生不接受新的医疗补助患者。”“这突显了为什么提供包括不同种族、收入阶层或医疗保险地位的高质量生殖保健比以往任何时候都更加重要。”
成立于2018年的Twentyeight Health表示，将利用这笔新资金继续将其服务扩展到美国各地。
note_en:
The mission to provide women who are Medicaid or underinsured should not be deprived of the same kinds of care that patients who have more income security or better healthcare coverage enjoy, according to the company’s founder, Amy Fan.
 The mission, and the company’s technology, have managed to convince a slew of investors who have poured $5.1 million in seed funding into the new startup. Third Prime led the round, which included investments from Town Hall Ventures, SteelSky Ventures, Aglaé Ventures, GingerBread Capital, Rucker Park Capital, Predictive VC, and angel investors like Stu Libby, Zoe Barry, and Wan Li Zhu.
  “Women who are on Medicaid, who are underinsured or without health insurance often struggle to find access to reproductive health services, and these struggles have only increased with COVID-19 pandemic limiting access to in-person appointments,” said Amy Fan, co-founder of Twentyeight Health, in a statement. “We are fighting for healthcare equity, ensuring that all women, particularly BIPOC women and women from low-income backgrounds, can access high quality, dignified and convenient care.”
 To ensure that its catering to underserved communities, the company works with Bottomless Closet, a workforce entry program for women, and the 8 colleges in the City University of New York ecosystem including LaGuardia College, which has 45,000 students with 70% coming from families making less than $30,000 in annual income.
 The company’s services are currently available across Florida, Maryland, New York, New Jersey, North Carolina and Pennsylvania and it’s the only telemedicine company focused on contraception services to accept Medicaid.
 In another example of how awesome this company is, it’s also working to provide free birth control for women who aren’t able to pay out of pocket and are uninsured through a partnership with Bedsider’s Contraceptive Access Fund. The company also donates 2% of its revenue to Bedsider and the National Institute for Reproductive Health. (Y’all, this company is amaze.)
 To sign up for the service, new customers fill out a medical questionnaire online. Once the questionnaire is reviewed by a US board-certified doctor within 24 hours customers can access over 100 FDA-approved brands of birth control pills, patches, rings, shots, and emergency contraception and receive a shipment within three days.
 Twentyeight Health provides ongoing care through online audio consultations and doctor follow up messages to discuss issues around updating prescriptions or addressing side effects, the company said.
 “Today, low-income women are three times more likely to have an unintended pregnancy than the average woman in the U.S., and nearly one-third of physicians nationwide aren’t accepting new Medicaid patients,” said Bruno Van Tuykom, co-founder of Twentyeight Health, in a statement. “This underscores why offering high-quality reproductive care that is inclusive of people across race, income bracket, or health insurance status is more important than ever.”
 Launched in 2018, Twentyeight Health said it would use the new cash to continue to expand its services across the U.S.
title: Twentyeight Health是一家远程医疗公司，扩大了妇女健康和生殖保健的机会
title_en: Twentyeight Health is a telemedicine company expanding access to women’s health and reproductive care

这家假媒体有一篇文章，鼓励人们发送密码并获得双倍返还，作为一种营销策略。然后，它链接到子页面以获得进一步的说明。
此外，隐秘兰德、亚历山大·斯维茨基、利兹·克林格、奈特·鲁本，你们在这个中等克隆人中被模仿。
这里最明显的攻击载体是找到一个之前经过验证的Twitter帐户，并更改其名称。那个小小的蓝丝带表情符号看起来很像是用户名的保证，不是吗？
note_en:
The youtube video is simply a link to a Cybertruck commercial. But the link in the image is a fairly nicely designed website impersonating Elon Musk’s Medium page.
 The fake medium has one article, encouraging people to send crypto and receive double back as a marketing ploy. It then links off to sub pages for further instruction.
  Further,  Crypto Rand,  Aleksandar Svetski,  Liz Klinger,  Nate Ruben, you’re being impersonated at this medium clone.

note:
一项研究发现，自1995年以来，由于气候变化导致的海洋变暖，澳大利亚的大堡礁已经失去了一半以上的珊瑚。
科学家们发现，全世界最大的珊瑚礁系统中，所有类型的珊瑚都在减少。
最陡峭的下降发生在2016年和2017年的大规模漂白事件之后。今年发生了更多的大规模漂白。
研究人员说，没有时间可以浪费了--我们必须尽快大幅减少温室气体排放。
这项研究发表在“英国皇家学会学报B”上，由昆士兰ARC珊瑚礁研究卓越中心的海洋科学家进行。
从1995年到2017年，科学家们评估了整个珊瑚礁的珊瑚群落的健康状况和大小。
他们发现，在所有珊瑚大小和物种中，种群数量都下降了50%以上，但分枝珊瑚和桌状珊瑚的数量下降了更多。
这些是大型的结构性物种，通常为鱼类和其他海洋生物提供栖息地。
这项研究的合著者特里·休斯教授说，这些珊瑚类型受到背靠背大规模漂白的影响最严重，造成三分之二的珊瑚礁受损。
当珊瑚在压力下赶走赋予它们颜色的藻类--被称为虫黄藻--时，就会发生漂白。
2019年的一项研究发现，由于大多数成年珊瑚已经死亡，受损的珊瑚群一直难以再生。
主要作者安迪·迪泽尔博士说，一个充满活力的珊瑚种群有数百万的小珊瑚幼崽，也有许多大的珊瑚。
我们的结果表明，与过去相比，大堡礁的恢复能力--它的弹性--受到了损害，因为现在的婴儿和繁殖的大型成鱼的数量都减少了。
去年，澳大利亚政府的珊瑚礁官方机构证实，人类活动导致的气候变暖仍然是珊瑚礁长期生存的最大威胁。
该珊瑚礁绵延2300公里(1400英里)，1981年因其巨大的科学和内在重要性而被指定为世界遗产。
但特别是在过去的十年里，它受到了温暖的海洋的巨大破坏，温暖的海洋杀死了珊瑚，驱散了其他海洋生物，加速了藻类和其他污染物的生长。
休斯教授表示：我们过去认为大堡礁受到其巨大规模的保护，但我们的结果表明，即使是世界上最大、保护相对较好的珊瑚礁系统也在日益受到破坏，而且正在衰落。
今年3月，科学家们报告说，该珊瑚礁在五年内遭受了第三次大规模白化事件。损害的全面程度仍在评估中。
自前工业化时代以来，全球气温已经上升了约1摄氏度。联合国警告说，如果上升到1.5摄氏度，世界上90%的珊瑚将会灭绝。
note_en:
Australia's Great Barrier Reef has lost more than half of its corals since 1995 due to warmer seas driven by climate change, a study has found.
 Scientists found all types of corals had suffered a decline across the world's largest reef system.
 The steepest falls came after mass bleaching events in 2016 and 2017. More mass bleaching occurred this year.
 "There is no time to lose - we must sharply decrease greenhouse gas emissions ASAP," the researchers said.
 The study, published in the journal Proceedings of the Royal Society B, was conducted by marine scientists at the ARC Centre of Excellence for Coral Reef Studies in Queensland.
 Scientists assessed the health and size of coral colonies across the reef from 1995 to 2017.
  They found populations had dropped by more than 50% in all coral sizes and species, but especially in branching and table-shaped corals.
 These are the large, structural species which usually provide habitats for fish and other marine life.
  Prof Terry Hughes, a study co-author, said these coral types had been "worst affected" by the back-to-back mass bleachings which damaged two-thirds of the reef.
 Bleaching occurs when corals under stress drive out the algae - known as zooxanthellae - that give them colour.
  A study in 2019 found that  damaged coral colonies had struggled to regenerate because most of the adult corals had died.
 "A vibrant coral population has millions of small, baby corals, as well as many large ones," said lead author Dr Andy Dietzel.
     "Our results show the ability of the Great Barrier Reef to recover - its resilience - is compromised compared to the past, because there are fewer babies, and fewer large breeding adults."
  Last year, the Australian government's official agency on the reef confirmed that human-driven warming remained the biggest threat to the reef's long-term survival.
 Stretching over 2,300km (1,400 miles), the reef was designated a World Heritage site in 1981 for its "enormous scientific and intrinsic importance".
 But in the past decade in particular, it has been vastly damaged by warmer seas which have killed off coral, dispersed other sea life and sped up growth of algae and other contaminants.
  "We used to think the Great Barrier Reef is protected by its sheer size - but our results show that even the world's largest and relatively well-protected reef system is increasingly compromised and in decline," said Prof Hughes.
 In March, scientists reported the reef had suffered its third mass bleaching event in five years. The full scale of the damage was still being assessed.
 Global temperatures have already risen by about 1C since pre-industrial times.  The UN has warned that if that rise reaches 1.5C, 90% of the world's corals will be wiped out.
title: 自1995年以来，大堡礁已经失去了一半的珊瑚
title_en: Great Barrier Reef has lost half of its corals since 1995

也许Roku设备目前最大的实际缺点是缺乏对HBO Max的支持。这应该会在晚些时候到来，但到目前为止，还没有一个方便的方法来绕过它。如果这对你来说很重要，你可能会想买亚马逊的Fire TV Stick 4K，它也不正式支持HBO Max(或孔雀)，但可以附带加载一些应用程序。Fire TV Stick 4K还支持杜比视觉HDR，这是这些Roku设备所缺乏的。亚马逊的流媒体服务周二以30美元的价格出售，但这笔交易似乎已经到期。
这里有一个简单的办法：如果你在黄金日结束前(美国东部时间10月15日凌晨3点)购买了至少40美元的亚马逊礼品卡，或者将现有的礼品卡余额至少充值40美元，亚马逊表示，它将免费再投入10美元的促销积分。
您必须通过一两封电子邮件才能兑换积分，每个帐户只能兑换一次优惠，而且积分仅适用于亚马逊自己销售和发货的产品。你们还必须在11月29日之前兑现信用证。但是如果你无论如何都打算在假期前买一张礼品卡，这是一个不错的小奖励。
购买40美元或更多的亚马逊礼品卡，或重新充值40美元或更多的礼品卡余额，即可获得10美元的促销积分(使用代码：3GC20PRIME)。
黄金周并没有带来大量的重磅视频游戏交易--我们预计在黑色星期五前后会有更多此类交易--但也有一些值得注意的报价。例如，许多第一方的任天堂Switch游戏的价格都降到了40美元，包括必需品，比如《塞尔达传奇：野性的呼吸》、《超级马里奥·奥德赛》、《超级马里奥派对》和战术RPG《火徽：三栋房子》。
并非所有这些交易都是历史最低水平，但鉴于任天堂在打折自己的游戏方面出了名的吝啬，它们的价格仍高于我们在一年中绝大多数时间里通常看到的价格。更好的是，许多优惠不需要Prime。
与此同时，在PlayStation方面，戏剧性的(也是具有争议性的)《最后的我们2》(The Last Of Us Part II)也跌至40美元，这与今年6月上市的一款流行游戏迄今的最好价格持平。请注意，它将可以在即将到来的PS5上播放。
在服务方面，订阅微软Xbox Live Gold三个月的服务--在线玩大多数Xbox游戏仍然需要该服务--优惠10美元。索尼的PlayStation Now服务一年只需18美元，你可以下载或云串流相当广泛的游戏库。另一项交易将为期12个月的任天堂Switch在线家庭会员与推荐的128 GB SanDisk microSD卡捆绑在一起，售价40美元。鉴于上述家庭会员费通常为35美元，而且交换机本身只包含32 GB的内置存储空间，这是一个不错的价格。
最后，我们仍然看到一些游戏机和个人电脑的游戏配件有不错的优惠。我们之前已经测试并吹捧轻量级的Razer Viper是一款很棒的游戏鼠标。我们还可以担保其他一些打折产品，比如PowerA的Moga手机游戏夹，它非常适合较新的云游戏服务。
任天堂Switch在线12个月家庭会员+SanDisk128 GB microSD卡用于任天堂Switch，售价39.99美元(通常为70美元)。
HyperX ChargePlay Duo为Xbox无线控制器和Xbox Elite无线控制器提供双充电站，售价22.99亿美元(通常为25美元)。
黄金日看起来是购买新microSD卡的好时机，因为256 GB版本的三星EVO Select已经跌至28美元的新低。我们以前推荐过这一款：它的速度没有好的V90卡快，但对于增强交换机或Android手机存储而言，它带来的大容量和足够好的性能让它物有所值。这款卡的速度没有好的V90卡快，但对于增强交换机或Android手机的存储来说，它的价值很高，因为它提供了足够大的容量和足够好的性能。
与此同时，摄影师和视频制作人可能想要注意下面的Lexar Professional UHS-II SD卡。与更常见的UHS-I卡相比，这些卡具有更高的读/写时间，非常适合记录和卸载大型4K视频文件或数百张原始照片。实际上，我去年就做了一笔类似的交易-今年还需要再次这么做，因为不幸的是，我失去了我的。
谈到智能家居设备，张伯伦畅销的MyQ车库开门器有了一个新的最低价，它可以让你和最多三个人通过你的手机通过Wi-Fi打开你的车库。在室内，亚马逊的Echo Show 5智能显示屏和(上一代)Echo Dot扬声器与我们之前强调的许多其他亚马逊设备交易相比，仍然有很大的折扣。
最后，是的，这里有一款售价超过50美元的产品：Satechi紧凑型背光蓝牙键盘。请原谅我们有点作弊，不过，打折20美元，这是我们见过的最划算的通用三设备无线键盘，在我们最新的在家办公设备指南的无线键盘部分中，它位居榜首。Razer基站Chroma耳机支架也因其RGB造型和USB端口可扩展性而入选该榜单，Satechi的铝制USB耳机支架也是如此；这两款耳机支架也都方便地跌至黄金日的新低。

适用于任天堂Switch的SanDisk microSDXC卡(256 GB)：1-UHS-I，U3，V3，售价42.99美元(通常为54美元)。

note_en:
Amazon's two-day Prime Day event is kicking off its last day, and we’ve been diligently combing through it all for the best deals. While there are some higher-priced products seeing steep discounts—see our full  Prime Day deals roundup for the details—we have a handful of our favorite deals under the $50 mark that'll reward thriftiness.
 Among them are desktop accessories, streaming devices, microSD cards, and a few more fun choices, like Nintendo Switch games and smart home gear.
 While you may see some shipping delays on some picks due to Prime Day's high demand, you can check out the full list below to nab yourself some cheap thrills.
   Roku's Streaming Stick+ and Premiere are good options for getting your home theater on, particularly if you need 4K and HDR support. Between the two, the Roku Streaming Stick+ has voice control and power/mute buttons built into its remote, as well as faster 802.11ac Wi-Fi. The Roku Premiere also isn't a "stick" design, so it can't disappear behind your TV quite as easily. But if you don't have many wireless issues at home and want to save $10, both devices run the same easy-to-use (if  aesthetically dated) Roku interface, open apps quickly, and support the same streaming services. It's also possible to hook up either device to an Echo speaker  for voice control via Alexa.
 Perhaps the biggest practical drawback to Roku devices right now is their lack of HBO Max support. That should come at a later date, but as of right now there isn't a convenient way around it. If that's important to you, you may want to grab  Amazon's Fire TV Stick 4K instead, which doesn't officially support HBO Max (or Peacock) either but can sideload the apps with  a little legwork. The Fire TV Stick 4K also supports Dolby Vision HDR, which these Roku devices lack. Amazon's streamer  was on sale for $30 on Tuesday, but the deal appears to have since expired.
   Here's an easy one: If you pick up at least $40 in Amazon.com gift cards—or reload an existing gift card balance with at least $40—before Prime Day ends (at 3am ET on October 15), Amazon says it'll toss in another $10 of promotional credit at no extra cost.
 You'll have to go through an email or two to redeem the credit, the offer can only be redeemed once per account, and the credit will only apply to products sold and shipped by Amazon itself. You'll also have to redeem the credit by November 29. But if you plan to pick up a gift card before the holidays anyway, it's a nice little bonus.
 Get  $10 promo credit when you buy an Amazon gift card of $40 or more or reload a gift card balance with $40 or more (use code:  GC20PRIME).
   Prime Day hasn't brought a ton of blockbuster video game deals—we expect more of those around Black Friday—but there are a few offers worth noting. A number of first-party Nintendo Switch games, for instance, are down to $40, including essentials like   The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild,   Super Mario Odyssey,  Super Mario Party, and the tactical RPG  Fire Emblem: Three Houses.
 Not all of these deals are all-time lows, but, given that Nintendo is famously stingy about discounting its own titles, they're still better prices than we typically see over the vast majority of the year. Better yet, many of the offers do not require Prime.
 Over on the PlayStation side, meanwhile, the dramatic ( and divisive)  The Last of Us Part II is also down to $40, which matches the best price to date on a popular game that arrived this past June. Note that it  will be playable on the upcoming PS5.
 In terms of services, a three-month subscription to Microsoft's Xbox Live Gold—which is still required to play most Xbox games online—is $10 off. A year of  Sony's PlayStation Now service, which lets you download or cloud-stream a  reasonably wide library of games, is $18 off. Another deal bundles a 12-month Nintendo Switch Online family membership with a  recommended 128GB SanDisk microSD card for $40. Given that said family membership normally goes for $35 and the Switch itself only includes 32GB of built-in storage, that's a good value.
 Finally, we're still seeing a few good offers on gaming accessories for both consoles and PCs. We've  previously tested and touted the lightweight Razer Viper as a great gaming mouse, for one. We can vouch for a few other discounted products, too, such as PowerA's Moga mobile gaming clip, which fits nicely with  newer cloud gaming services.
 Nintendo Switch Online 12-month Family Membership + SanDisk 128GB microSD Card for Nintendo Switch for  $39.99 (normally $70).
  HyperX ChargePlay Duo dual charging station for Xbox Wireless Controller and Xbox Elite Wireless Controllers for  $22.99 (normally $25).
   Prime Day looks like a good time to pick up a new microSD card, as the 256GB version of Samsung's EVO Select is down to a new low of $28. We've  recommended this one before: it's not as fast as a  good V90 card, but for boosting Switch or Android phone storage, it's good value for the large capacity and good-enough performance it gives back.
 Photographers and video creators, meanwhile, might want to take note of the Lexar Professional UHS-II SD cards below. Meeting higher read/write times than the more common UHS-I cards, these are great for recording and offloading large 4K video files or hundreds of RAW photos in a snap. I actually jumped on a similar deal last year—and will need to again this year because, sadly, I lost mine.
 Moving to smart home gear, we have a new lowest price on Chamberlain's popular MyQ garage door opener, which enables you and up to three people to open your garage through your phone via Wi-Fi. For inside the house, Amazon's Echo Show 5 smart display and (last-gen) Echo Dot speaker are still deeply discounted alongside the many other Amazon device deals  we highlighted previously.
 Finally, yes, there's one product here that's over $50: the Satechi Compact Backlit Bluetooth Keyboard. Forgive us for cheating a bit, but, at $20 off, it's the best price we've seen for a universal three-device wireless keyboard that  topped the list in our working-from-home gear guide's wireless keyboard section. The Razer Base Station Chroma headphone stand  made that list for its RGB styling and USB-port extensibility, too, as did Satechi's Aluminum USB Headphone Stand; both are conveniently down to new lows for Prime Day as well.
  Samsung EVO Select (256GB) microSDXC card - UHS-I, U3, Class 10 for  $27.99 (normally $35).
 SanDisk microSDXC Card for Nintendo Switch (256GB) - UHS-I, U3, V3 for  $42.99 (normally $54).
   Lexar Professional 2000x (32GB) SDHC card - UHS-II, U3, V90 for  $34.99 ( 64GB model for $55 - normally $46).
             Aukey CB-CL02 (3.9ft, MFi certified) braided nylon USB-C to Lightning cable for  $9.59 (normally $12).

这两位佐治亚理工学院毕业生与帕特里克的兄弟丹尼尔·乔普森(Daniel Chopson)一起开发了一套软件产品，目前已被全球22个国家的数千名建筑师、工程师、承包商和开发人员使用，如EYP、P2S、Skanska和JLL。该公司的软件还在加州理工州立大学、伊利诺伊大学和北卡罗来纳大学夏洛特分校等大学教授，还有他们的母校佐治亚理工学院。
现在，在洛杉矶投资公司Mucker Capital牵头的首轮融资结束后，该公司的财富增加了570万美元，其中包括之前的投资者Urban.us、Knoll Ventures和亚特兰大自己的TechSquare Labs。
该公司的第一个产品是帮助对建筑物的能源消耗进行建模的软件，并提供关于如何提高能源效率的见解。根据cove.tool的说法，该产品将过去涉及外部顾问和大约150小时工作的手动过程转变为30分钟就可以完成的工作。
该软件可以考虑新建和现有建筑的能耗、光照、眩光、辐射、水和具体碳目标等因素，并提供比较不同选择的能力，使建筑师和开发商能够确定实现能源目标的最具成本效益的方式。在最近的更新中，该公司增加了一个入住工具，以帮助开发人员了解最安全的设计，以减少新冠肺炎等空气传播疾病的潜在传播。
根据全球建筑与建设联盟(Global Alliance For Building And Construction)和国际能源署(International Energy Agency)发布的数据，建筑物和建筑施工是导致气候变化的温室气体排放的巨大贡献者，每年约占碳排放的39%。持续的全球城市移民意味着对新建筑和建筑的需求不会在短期内放缓。该公司表示，随着对建筑需求的增加，Cove.tool的软件等技术可以在一个典型的建筑项目中节省相当于4万棵树的成本。
该公司首席执行官阿胡贾(Ahuja)表示：“我们只有大约10年的时间将建筑物降低到实际上为零，然后这一行动在阻止气候变化方面就会毫无用处。”
据Ahuja称，有了新的资金，cove.tool打算扩大全球销售和营销努力，并开发一些新项目。两位创始人都表示，该软件的设计已经符合加拿大、英国和澳大利亚的建筑标准。该公司还计划看看是否能为火星环境设计节能结构。
“为了好玩，我们要去火星，”阿胡加说。“我们想看看模型是什么样子的。”
该软件的一大卖点是，环境可持续性被融入到产品中，所以即使开发人员只关心削减成本，他们无论如何都会改善他们的碳足迹。
Ahuja说：“每个使用我们平台的开发者可能关心可持续性，也可能不关心可持续性，但他们肯定会节省成本。”
产品路线图上的下一步是一个可以提供节能材料的市场，施工经理和开发人员需要这些材料来将Cove.Tool设计转化为实际的建筑。
“每个人都在使用一种完全不同的糟糕的工作流程，”该公司的联合创始人和产品开发负责人肖普森(Chopson)说。“就成本和抵消碳排放目标而言，这是每个建筑和每个城市实际上都需要达到的目标。”
路线图是创建从架构师到承包商的更简单的工作流程，以便参与的每个人都能更紧密地协调。当Cove.tool进入建筑市场的这一边时，它会发现自己面临着一些资金雄厚的竞争对手，但这是因为建筑管理和采购市场的规模很大。
像ProCore这样的公司已经成为价值数十亿美元的企业。他们的主张是简化施工管理流程。
据Ahuja称，Cove.Tool市场产品将在2021年年中的某个时候到达，该公司已经积累了一个数据库，其中包含来自数百家供应商的1000多种产品，并打算上市。
“有很多产品数据库，但没有人能对其进行分析，”肖普森说。“我们是唯一能分析这种玻璃比其他任何玻璃都好的人。”它是高度杂乱无章的，你不能把一件事和另一件事进行比较。关键是要能够分析事物，并把你所做的分析放在一栋建筑的背景下。“
创始人表示，最终，重点仍将是效率和可持续性。在一个迅速变暖的世界里，几乎没有什么事情是重要的。
正如Mucker Capital的合伙人、cove.tool董事会新任董事奥马尔·哈穆伊(Omar Hamoui)在一份声明中所说，“可持续设计正迅速成为建筑界的必需品。”
note_en:
Along with Patrick’s brother, Daniel Chopson, the two Georgia Institute of Technology graduates have developed a suite of software products that are now used by thousands of architects, engineers, contractors and developers like EYP, P2S, Skanska, and JLL in 22 countries around the world. The company’s software is also taught in universities including California Polytechnic State University, the University of Illinois, and UNC Charlotte, along with their alma matter, Georgia Tech.
 Now the company is $5.7 million richer following the close of its series A funding led by the Los Angeles-based investment firm Mucker Capital and including previous investors Urban.us, Knoll Ventures, and Atlanta’s own Techsquare Labs.
  The company’s first product is software that helps model the energy consumption of a building and provides insights on how to improve energy efficiency. The product turns what used to be a manual process that involved outside consultants and roughly 150 hours of work into a job that can be done in 30 minutes, according to the cove.tool.
 The software can account for factors such as energy consumption, light exposure, glare, radiation, water and embodied carbon targets for new and existing buildings and offers the ability to compare different options, allowing architects and developers to determine the most cost-efficient way to meet energy targets. In its most recent update, the company added an occupancy tool to help developers understand the safest designs for reducing the potential spread of airborne diseases like COVID-19.
 Buildings and building construction are a huge contributor to the greenhouse gas emissions that contribute to climate change, accounting for roughly  39 percent of carbon emissions annually, according to data released by the Global Alliance for Building and Construction and the International Energy Agency. And the continuing global migration to cities means that demand for new buildings and construction won’t slow down anytime soon. As demand for buildings increases, technologies like cove.tool’s software could save the equivalent of 40,000 trees on a typical construction project, the company said.
  “ We only have about 10 years to lower buildings to actually be net zero before the action would be useless in terms of stopping climate change,” said Ahuja, the company’s chief executive.
 With the new funds in hand cove.tool intends to expand global sales and marketing efforts and develop some new projects, according to Ahuja. Both founders said that the software is already designed to meet the building standards for Canada, the United Kingdom and Australia. And the company has a plan to see if it can design energy efficient structures for a martian environment.
 “For fun, we’re going to do Mars,” Ahuja said. “We want to see what the model looks like.”
 The big selling point for the software is that environmental sustainability is baked into the product so even if developers only care about cost-cutting, they’ll be improving their carbon footprint anyway.
 “Every developer that uses our platform may or may not care about sustainability, but they definitely save on cost,” said Ahuja.
 Next on the product roadmap is a marketplace that can provide energy efficient materials that construction managers and developers would need to turn the cove.tool designs into actual buildings.
 “Everybody is using a completely different bad workflow,” Chopson, the company’s co-founder and product development lead, said. “This brings it together in terms of cost and the offset carbon targets that every building and every city actually need to meet.”
 The roadmap is to create easier workflows from the architect to the contractor so everyone involved can coordinate more closely. As it moves into this side of the construction market, cove.tool will find itself facing some very well-funded competitors, but that’s because the construction management and procurement side of the market is massive.
 Companies like Procore have become billion dollar businesses on the back of. their pitch to simplify the construction management process.
 The cove.tool marketplace product will be arriving sometime in the middle of 2021 and the company has already amassed a database of over 1,000 products from hundreds of vendors that it intends to list, according to Ahuja.
 “There’s a lot of product databases, but no one can analyze it,” said Chopson. “We’re the only ones who can analyze that glass is better than any other glass.. It’s highly disorganized and you can’t compare one thing versus another.. The key is to be able to analyze things and put the analysis you do in the context of a building.”
 Ultimately, the focus will still be on efficiency and sustainability, the founders said. And in a rapidly warming world, there are few things that are important.
 As Omar Hamoui, a partner at Mucker Capital and the new director on the cove.tool board, said in a statement, “Sustainable design is rapidly becoming a necessity in the built world.”
title: 推销技术以优化建筑设计以实现可持续性，总部位于亚特兰大的Cove.tool筹集了570万美元

欧盟主席乌苏拉·冯德莱恩(Ursula Von Der Leyen)已将一项绿色协议作为其欧盟政策计划的关键内容-其长期目标是到2050年实现该地区的“气候中立”。与此同时，工厂化农业仍然是温室气体的巨大贡献者-这意味着有必要重新考虑欧洲如何生产食物和人们吃什么。增加对可再生能源的投资和改善建筑隔热性能(欧盟委员会也承诺这样做)不足以实现关键的气候目标。因此，地区性企业围绕肉类替代品进行创新的机会越来越大-无论是实验室培育的肉类还是植物性蛋白质。
Meat4All项目在8月初获得了Horizon 2020的拨款，但现在才刚刚宣布。总部位于圣塞巴斯蒂安的生物技术食品公司一直在生产一种基于猪肉细胞的免屠宰产品，名为ethicameat sicne 2017，该公司是该财团的领头羊。
该项目的目标是“有竞争力的、可持续的和以消费者为导向的替代动物蛋白来源的产业化和商业化”，他们的建议侧重于提高养殖肉类生产技术；致力于市场接受度；以及测试以评估安全性，以将更多养殖肉类产品推向市场。
生物技术食品公司首席执行官Iñigo Charola在一份声明中评论说：“对于生物技术食品公司的整个团队和我们的有机技术公司的合作伙伴来说，为我们的‘Meat4All’项目获得欧盟的支持是非常令人满意的。这是欧洲第一次有效地致力于养殖肉类。养殖肉类将是我们未来饮食的关键成分，现在我们也得到了机构的确认。“。
该财团的主要目标包括将养殖肉类的产量从公斤扩大到吨；保持大规模的营养价值；获得不含动物血清的细胞培养手段；以及使用未经转基因的动物细胞。
其他声明的目标包括开发有竞争力的产品和进行品尝测试，以确定和预测市场需求。
他们还在一份新闻稿中写道：“通过推广这项技术，‘Meat4All’将创建一个新的发展领域，通过在整个欧盟范围内促进竞争力和创造增长，使欧洲行业能够利用这个市场的高潜力。”他们补充说：“挑战不是别人，而是达到供应肉类加工业所需的生产能力。”
note_en:
EU president  Ursula von der Leyen has made a Green Deal a key plank of her policy plan for the bloc — with the long term aim of the region being “climate-neutral” by 2050. At the same time factory farming remains a massive contributor of greenhouse gases — meaning there’s an imperative to rethink how Europe produces food and what people eat. Boosting investment in renewable energy and improving building insulation (which the Commission has also pledged to do) won’t be enough to meet key climate targets. So there’s growing opportunity for regional businesses to innovate around meat alternatives — whether that’s lab-grown meat or  plant-based proteins.
 The Meat4All project was awarded the Horizon 2020  grant at the start of August but it’s just being announced now. San Sebastián-based  BioTech Foods, which has been producing a slaughter-free pork-cell based product called  Ethicameat sicne 2017, is leading the consortium.
   The aim of the project is the “Industrialization and commercialization of a competitive, sustainable and consumer oriented alternative animal protein source”, with their proposal focused on increasing cultured meat production technology; working on market acceptance; and testing to assess safety to bring more cultured meat products to market.
 Commenting in a statement, Iñigo Charola, CEO of BioTech Foods, said: “It is hugely satisfying for the entire team at BioTech Foods, and for our partners at Organotechnie, to obtain this backing from the European Union for our ‘Meat4All’ project. This is the first time that Europe has effectively committed to cultured meat. Cultured meat will be a key ingredient of our future diet, and now we have it confirmed also by the institutions.”
 Key aims for the consortium include scaling up production of cultured meat from kilograms to tonnes; maintaining the nutritional value for large-scale amounts; obtaining the means to culture cells free from animal serum; and the use of animal cells that have not been genetically modified.
 Other stated aims include developing a competitive product and performing taste tests to determine and predict market demand.
 “By extending this technology, ‘Meat4All’ will create a new development area which will enable the European industry to leverage the high potential of this market, by fostering competitiveness and creating growth throughout the European Union,” they also write in a press release, adding: “The challenge is none other than to reach the production capacity necessary to supply the meat processing industry.”
title: 实验室培育的肉类项目首次尝到欧盟公共资金的滋味
title_en: Lab-grown meat project gets first taste of EU public funds

谷歌网络分析平台的最新版本名为谷歌分析4(Google Analytics 4)，现已面世。新的属性类型包括扩展的预测洞察力、与谷歌广告的更深入集成、跨设备测量功能和更精细的数据控制。
周三宣布的这一版本是对去年推出的App+Web属性的扩展和品牌重塑，标志着超越Universal Analytics迈出了重要的第一步。
新的人工智能支持的洞察力和预测。虽然分析中的机器学习洞察力已经有一段时间了，但进入谷歌分析4的新洞察力和预测可以自动提醒营销者注意数据趋势，比如对他们销售的产品的需求激增。
这项技术还用于预测结果，例如流失率和企业可以从特定客户群中赚取的潜在收入。这些洞察力可以帮助营销人员预测他们的客户未来可能采取的行动，并将重点放在更高价值的受众身上。
更深入的受众与谷歌广告的融合。营销人员可以通过网络和他们的应用程序从他们的访问者那里建立和维护受众。例如，如果用户由于在网络上采取的操作而有资格进入受众列表，并因为在应用程序内完成了购买而被从列表中删除，则该列表将自动更新以删除该用户，这样他们就不会被广告重定向。
此外，Google Analytics 4将报告发生在应用程序内和网络上的YouTube参与视图的操作。
客户生命周期-框架报告。Analytics 4和Universal Analytics之间最显著的区别之一是报告的组织方式。
谷歌分析集团产品经理罗素·凯彻姆(Russell Ketchum)告诉搜索引擎王国(Search Engine Land)：“我们重新组织了App+Web测试版中的所有报告，增加了一些新功能，现在它是围绕客户生命周期组织的。”
这些报告旨在帮助营销人员深入了解客户之旅的特定方面。该公司表示：“例如，你可以在用户获取报告中看到哪些渠道正在吸引新客户，然后使用参与度和留存报告来了解这些客户在转换后采取的行动，以及他们是否会留下来。”
新的Analytics体验还旨在让营销人员更全面地了解客户是如何跨设备和渠道与他们的业务互动的。营销人员可以提供他们自己的用户ID，或者使Google Signals能够在不同设备上对用户进行重复数据删除，以便进行报告和广告定向。
无代码事件跟踪。扩展的无代码功能使营销人员更容易实时跟踪和测量重要的现场和应用内操作，如页面滚动或视频播放，而不必在Google Tag Manager中添加代码或设置事件跟踪。在Universal Analytics中，事件跟踪需要包括延迟在内的额外处理，并且数据通常要到第二天才可用。
更精细的用户数据控制。Analytics 4还包括帮助广告商遵守GDPR和CCPA等数据法规的选项。
同意模式是为必须获得最终用户同意才能收集分析数据的站点设计的。这一新模式为分析和广告提供了单独的同意选择加入。Ketchum说：“在之前的[Google Analytics]版本中没有这种粒度级别，我们看到客户所做的只是简单地将分析完全排除在外，所以对于给定的用户来说，Analytics产品对我们客户的价值并不在那里。”
数据删除功能也得到了改进，使企业能够顺应用户的删除请求，而不必删除更多不必要的数据。这些功能还将包括一个预览模式，供企业验证他们即将删除的数据。
无Cookie未来的分析。随着第三方Cookie的逐步淘汰，谷歌预计数据稀疏将成为新的常态。它将依靠机器学习来填补数据空白。
“通常情况下，我们将拥有一组混合的数据：我们将拥有事件数据，但不一定是与之相关联的用户标识符。凯彻姆说：“我们在数据上完全会有差距，所有的测量提供商都会出现这种情况。”他补充说，“我们今天没有任何关于这方面的具体公告，但随着我们进入明年，我们将使用机器学习建模来支持谷歌分析中的各种分析模式。”他补充说，“我们今天没有关于这一点的任何具体声明，但随着我们进入明年，我们将使用机器学习建模来支持谷歌分析中的各种分析模式。”
“我们将有能力拥有不同的模式，这些模式可能不那么强调用户分析方面，但更多地关注行为方面，”他举例说。
为什么我们会在乎。新的Google Analytics 4属性提供了客户生命周期的更完整的跨渠道视图，并将这些信息与预测性营销功能结合使用，为营销人员提供了更多信息和有效的方式来根据这些洞察力采取行动。
当您设置新属性时，Google Analytics 4将是默认选项。之前的迭代，Universal Analytics，将继续可用，但Google建议网站所有者设置这两种属性类型，并并行运行。新功能开发将集中在GA4上。
note_en:
The latest iteration of Google’s web analytics platform, dubbed Google Analytics 4, is now available. The new property type includes expanded predictive insights, deeper integration with Google Ads, cross-device measurement capabilities and more granular data controls.
  The release,  announced Wednesday, is an expansion and rebranding of the  App + Web property launched last year and marks the first major step in evolving beyond Universal Analytics.
 New AI-powered insights and predictions. While  machine learning-powered insights in Analytics have been available for some time, the new insights and predictions making their way to Google Analytics 4 can automatically alert marketers to data trends such as surging demand for a product they sell.
  This technology is also used to predict outcomes, such as churn rates and the potential revenue a business could earn from a particular segment of customers. Those insights can help marketers anticipate actions their customers might take in the future and focus on higher-value audiences.
 Deeper audiences integration with Google Ads. Marketers can build and maintain audiences from their visitors across the web and their app. If a user qualified for an audience list due to an action taken on the web and were taken off a list because they completed a purchase within the app, for example, the list would then be automatically updated to remove the user so that they’re not retargeted with ads.
  Additionally, Google Analytics 4 will report on actions from YouTube engaged views that occur in-app as well as on the web.
 Customer lifecycle-framed reporting. One of the most striking differences between Analytics 4 and Universal Analytics is how reports are organized.
 “We’ve reorganized all of the reports that had been in the App + Web beta, added a handful of additions, and now it’s organized around the customer lifecycle,'” Russell Ketchum, Group Product Manager, Google Analytics, told Search Engine Land.
 The reports are designed to help marketers drill down into particular aspects of the customer journey. “For example, you can see what channels are driving new customers in the user acquisition report, then use the engagement and retention reports to understand the actions these customers take, and whether they stick around, after converting,” the company said.
 The new Analytics experience also aims to give marketers a more complete view of how customers are engaging with their businesses across devices as well as channels. Marketers can provide Their own user ID or enable  Google signals to deduplicate users across devices for reporting and ad targeting.
 Codeless Event tracking. Expanded codeless features make it easier for marketers to track and measure on-site and in-app actions that matter — in real-time — such as a page scroll or video play, without having to add code or set up event tracking in Google Tag Manager. Within Universal Analytics, event tracking requires additional processing which includes latency, and the data is typically not available until the following day.
 More granular user data controls. Analytics 4 also includes options to help advertisers comply with data regulations such as  GDPR and  CCPA.
 Consent mode is designed for sites that have to obtain end-user consent to collect analytics data. This new mode provides separate consent opt-ins for analytics and ads. “Without this level of granularity in the previous version [of Google Analytics], what we were seeing customers do is simply exclude analytics wholesale, and so for that given user, the value of the Analytics product to our customer wasn’t there,” said Ketchum.
  Data deletion capabilities are also improved, enabling businesses to comply with deletion requests from users without having to remove more data than ncessary. These features will also include a preview mode for businesses to verify the data they’re about to remove.
 Analytics in a cookie-less future. As third-party cookies are phased out, Google anticipates that data sparsity will become the new norm. It will rely on machine learning to fill in the data gaps.
 “The norm is that we’re going to have a mixed set of data: We’ll have event data but not necessarily a user identifier associated with it. We’ll have gaps in data altogether and this is going to be true of all of all measurement providers,” Ketchum said, adding, “We don’t have any specific announcements on this today, but as we get into next year, we’re going to be using machine learning modeling to support various modes of analysis in Google Analytics.”
 “We’ll have the ability to have different modes that maybe emphasize the user analysis side of it less, but focus more on the behavioral,” he provided as an example.
 Why we care. The new Google Analytics 4 property provides a more complete cross-channel view of the customer lifecycle and puts that information to use with predictive marketing features, providing marketers with more information and efficient ways to act on those insights.
 Google Analytics 4 will be the default option when you set up a new property. The previous iteration, Universal Analytics, will continue to remain available, but Google recommends site owners set up both property types and run them in parallel. New feature development will be focused on GA4.
title: Google Analytics 4发布时具有人工智能支持的洞察力、与Google Adds的更深入集成、跨设备测量和更精细的数据控制
title_en: Google Analytics 4 launches with AI-powered insights, deeper integration with Google Ads, cross-device measurements, and more granular data controls

现在是晚上7点，你工作到很晚。你会被饥饿阵痛的轻柔隆隆声打断，这是一个明确的提醒，表明你还没有吃晚饭。几个街区外有一家新开的融合餐厅，你一直想去尝尝，但一直没有机会去。每次你经过这里，都会热闹非凡。所以你在Yelp上查一下餐厅，看看它是否值得你的时间和金钱。你启动这款应用程序并进行搜索，结果却被一个印有不祥的大惊叹号的警报击中：
R字。这是新的红字。你大吃一惊，几乎忘了你饿了。你的大脑会争先恐后地寻找可能的解释。会不会是工作人员对一家餐厅进行了种族分析？也许，就像星巴克一样，据称工作人员拒绝让碰巧是黑人的非顾客进入卫生间？然后你还记得，这些天来，融合菜肴本身经常是关于文化挪用的争论的导火索。也许这家餐厅只是因为它的存在而带有种族主义色彩？
谁知道呢。幸运的是，Yelp友好地提供了一篇新闻文章的链接，在那里你可以了解到这一事件。另一种胃部抱怨促使你坚持最初的使命，迅速找到食物。你稍后再做研究。取而代之的是，你从熟悉、稳定和快速的地方点外卖。你在麦当劳看到过推广特拉维斯·斯科特(Travis Scott)餐饮的广告，他刚刚赢得了BET的年度最佳说唱歌手，所以你不可能做出种族主义的用餐决定。就是麦当劳了。
这种情况并不太牵强，这要归功于Yelp，这家公司的整个业务都是基于对其他业务的众包评论。与其他许多陷入政治激进主义浪潮的科技公司一样，Yelp决定通过启用一项功能来反对种族主义，他们声称，这项功能将向消费者提供与“令人震惊的、带有种族色彩的行为”相关的企业信息，以帮助人们做出“更明智的支出决定”。
事实上，Yelp正在引入一个基本的社会信用体系，让企业主受到文化战争变幻莫测的影响。Yelp对争议并不陌生，此前曾卷入诉讼，指控其迫使公司为其平台上的广告付费，并在公司屈服之前扣留正面评论作为筹码。
Yelp很清楚自己的力量可以决定生意的成败。研究表明，Yelp评级越高，销售额就越高，与声誉往往更久负盛名的连锁店相比，独立餐厅的这种影响更大。这一点，再加上选择退出Yelp对于任何想要在日益增长的网络世界中取得成功的企业来说都是职业自杀的事实，促使许多所有者不情愿地参与Yelp的平台，将其作为做生意的必要成本。
在宣布这一消息之前，武器化的Yelp评论已经是困扰两党政治过道的一个众所周知的现象(我在这里已经写过了)。有报道称，弗吉尼亚州列克星敦的一家小酒馆Red Hen的老板因时任白宫新闻秘书萨拉·哈克比·桑德斯(Sarah Huckabee Sanders)与特朗普政府的关系而要求她及其家人离开体制时，餐厅的Yelp页面收到了数千条欺诈性的一星评论。这遭到了反特朗普支持者的五星级评论的强烈反对，将这家小酒馆的Yelp页面变成了一个政治战场。
通过发布一份官方通知，只不过是对种族主义行为的指控，Yelp正在加强将其评论武器化的不正当动机。根本没有办法判断一家企业是否是虚假指控的受害者，特别是考虑到社交媒体堆积如山的疯狂步伐。对于一个利润率微乎其微的行业来说，餐厅声誉受损的每一天都会使其接近财务崩溃的边缘。只要看看吉布森面包店(Gibson‘s Bakery)的案例，它就赢得了对奥伯林学院(Oberlin College)的诽谤诉讼，罪名是虚假指控和名誉损害。面包店老板抓获了几名试图偷酒的黑人学生并进行了追逐，学生活动人士将整个事件定性为种族恶化，并在大学的帮助下，组织了大规模抗议活动和在线活动，以抵制这家有134年历史的家族经营的小面包店。
更复杂的是，什么是种族主义行为，这个模糊的、不断扩大的概念。包括被指控“窃取黑人文化”的波巴茶店“Trap Tea”的老板吗？那家餐厅展示支持“错误的”政治候选人的牌子怎么办？作为对Yelp声明的回应，一个反法组织已经向其追随者发出呼吁，要求他们开始编制一份名单，列出那些同情警察或反对黑人生命也是(Black Lives Matter)运动的企业。当“沉默就是暴力”这样的口头禅变得越来越常见时，怎么会有人板着脸说这样的政策不会适得其反呢？
Yelp和谷歌今年夏天还推出了一项功能，允许公司以“黑人所有的企业”或“LGBT拥有的企业”的身份上市。在一个以自由交易为基础的市场繁荣的国家，标明所有者的种族或性取向，以告知消费者在那里购物的决定(或者不在那里！)。可能会导致我们支离破碎的社会进一步分裂。
当我们读到中国的社会信用体系使其公民陷入社会控制的反乌托邦噩梦时，我们理所当然地感到震惊。个人在一个全方位的监控系统中受到监控，从现实生活中的违规行为和消费者行为，到在“私人”社交网络上的行为。基于这些输入，算法会给出一个数字分数，反映每个人的可信度和美德，然后决定他们在社会上能做什么，不能做什么。如今，媒体经常会在没有正当程序的情况下，自行报道种族主义指控，那么由政府精英运营的社会信用体系与由暴徒运营的社会信用体系到底有什么不同呢？
显示评论
note_en:
It’s seven in the evening and you’re working late. You’re interrupted by the soft rumble of hunger pangs, an unmistakable reminder that you haven’t eaten dinner yet. There’s this newish fusion restaurant a couple of blocks away that you’ve been wanting to try, but haven’t had the chance to. Every time you’ve walked past, it’s buzzing with activity. So you look the restaurant up on Yelp to see if it’s worth your time and money. You launch the app and search, only to be hit with an  alert emblazoned with an ominously large exclamation point:
  The R word. It’s the new scarlet letter. You’re so taken aback that you almost forget that you’re hungry. Your mind races to find a possible explanation. Could it be that the staff  racially profiled a diner? Perhaps, like  Starbucks, the staff allegedly denied bathroom access to a non-customer who happened to be black? Then you remember that these days, fusion cuisine itself is often a flashpoint in  debates about cultural appropriation. Maybe the restaurant is just racist by virtue of its existence?
 Who knows. Luckily, Yelp has kindly provided a link to a news article where you can learn about the incident. Another stomach grumble urges you to stick to your original mission to find sustenance quickly. You’ll do the research later. Instead you order takeout from somewhere that’s familiar, consistent and fast. You’ve seen ads promoting the  Travis Scott meal at McDonald’s and he just won BET’s rapper of the year so there’s no way you’re making a racist dining decision. McDonald’s it is.
 This scenario that isn’t too far-fetched, thanks to Yelp, a company whose entire business is predicated on crowdsourced reviews about other businesses. Like many other tech firms caught up in the currents of the heightened climate of political activism, Yelp has decided to take a stance against racism by enabling a feature that they claim would arm consumers with information about businesses associated with ‘ egregious, racially-charged actions’ to help people make ‘more informed spending decisions.’
  In reality, Yelp is introducing a rudimentary social credit system that subjects business owners to the vagaries of the culture wars. No stranger to controversy, Yelp has previously been embroiled in  lawsuits alleging that it forced companies to pay for advertising on its platform and withheld positive reviews as leverage until they gave in.
 Yelp is well aware of its own power to make or break businesses. Research  shows that higher Yelp ratings lead to higher sales, and that this effect is greater for independent restaurants as opposed to chains whose reputations tend to be more well-established. This, combined with the fact that opting out of Yelp is professional suicide for any business who wants to succeed in an increasingly online world, propels many owners to grudgingly participate on Yelp’s platform as a necessary cost of doing business.
 Prior to this announcement, weaponized Yelp reviews were already a well-known phenomenon (I’ve written about it  here) plaguing both sides of the political aisle. When it was reported that the owner of the Red Hen, a small bistro in Lexington, Virginia, had  asked then White House press secretary Sarah Huckabee Sanders and her family to leave the establishment due to her association with the Trump administration, the restaurant’s Yelp page received thousands of fraudulent one-star reviews. This was met with a backlash of anti-Trump supporters’ five-star reviews, transforming the bistro’s Yelp page into a political battleground.
 By injecting an official notice based on no more than an  accusation of racist behavior, Yelp is enhancing the perverse incentives to weaponize its reviews. There is simply no way to tell if a business is the victim of false accusations, especially given the frenzied pace of social media pile-ons. For an industry that runs on razor-thin margins, every day that a restaurant’s reputation is sullied is a day that edges it closer to financial ruin. Just look to the case of  Gibson’s Bakery, which won a libel suit over Oberlin College for false accusations and reputational damage. After the bakery owners caught a handful of black students attempting to steal liquor and gave chase, student activists framed the entire incident as a racial aggravation and organized, with the help of the university, sweeping protests against and online campaigns to boycott the small, 134-year old family-owned bakery.
  Further complications involve the vague, ever-expanding concept of what constitutes racist behavior. Does it include the owners of ‘ Trap Tea’, the boba tea shop who was accused of ‘stealing black culture’? What about the restaurant displaying a sign in support of the ‘wrong’ political candidate? Already, in response to Yelp’s statement, an antifa group has put out a  call to its followers to begin compiling a list of businesses guilty of showing sympathy for the police or that stand against the Black Lives Matter movement. As mantras such as ‘silence is violence’ become more commonplace, how can anyone say with a straight face that such a policy will not backfire?
 Yelp and Google also introduced a feature this summer allowing companies to list as a ‘Black-Owned Business’ or ‘LGBT-Owned Business’. In a country where markets thrive based on free exchange, flagging the race or sexuality of the owner to inform a consumer’s decision to shop there (or not!) could cause our riven society to divide further.
 We’re rightfully alarmed when we read about China’s social credit system that ensnares its own citizens in a dystopian nightmare of social control. Individuals are monitored in an all-encompassing surveillance system, from real-life transgressions and consumer behavior to conduct on ‘private’ social networks. Based on these inputs, algorithms spit out a numerical score that reflects each person’s trustworthiness and virtue, which then determines what they can and cannot do in society. Now given that these days, the media will often run a story about an accusation of racism on its own, without any due process, what exactly is the difference between a social credit system run by the elites in government versus one run by the mob?

title: Yelp的反种族主义社会信用噩梦

note: 在我1996年出版的“Tog on Software Design”一书中，我提出了Tog的通勤法则：通勤时间是固定的。只有距离是可变的。翻译？无论采取什么措施来降低复杂性，人们都会努力在他们的生活中体验到同等或更高水平的复杂性。道路越走越快，人们总是会搬得更远。把特斯勒的复杂性守恒定律和托格的通勤定律结合起来，你就会开始看到我们在未来必须预料到的二阶效应。如果人们坚持保持同等的复杂性，但我们降低了人们在给定任务中经历的复杂性，人们将承担更具挑战性的任务。这如何解释原始网络操作系统的扭曲比例呢？这些任务非常简单，因此即使由于缺少错误检查、不可见导航等而使完成这些任务变得更加困难，但总体复杂性并不比在功能更强大的操作系统(纯HTML界面GUI界面)中完成的复杂得多的任务域更难
note_en: In my 1996 book,  Tog on Software Design, I presented Tog's Law of Commuting: "The time of a commute is fixed. Only the distance is variable." Translation? People will strive to experience an equal or increasing level of complexity in their lives no matter what is done to reduce it. Make the roads faster, and people will invariably move further away.   								Combine Tesler's Law of Conservation of Complexity with Tog's Laws of Commuting and you begin to see the 2nd order effect that we must anticipate in the future. If people will insist on maintaining equal complexity, yet we reduce the complexity people experience in a given task, people will take on a more challenging task.   								How does that explain the skewed proportions of the original web "OS"? The tasks were dead-simple, so even though accomplishing them was made more difficult through lack of error-checking, invisible navigation, etc., the overall complexity was no more difficult than for a far more complex task domain accomplished in a more powerful OS:      								Pure HTML Interface      								GUI Interface
title: 复杂性悖论(1998)

note:
根据一项新的研究，患有长期COVID；的人发现自己太疲惫了，甚至连简单的任务都做不了。
一些在新冠肺炎感染后出现持续而持久的症状的人，在病毒最严重的几个月后几乎卧床不起。
这项发表在英国全科医学杂志上的研究，采访了24名长期COVID患者，了解他们的症状和缺乏可用的帮助。
接受采访的人中没有一人在最初怀疑或确认感染期间入院治疗。
由于在大流行的前几个月对病毒的社区测试有限，一半的研究参与者没有接受新冠肺炎的测试-而在那些进行了测试的人中，到那时只有一人的结果呈阳性。
一名据信在3月份患上这种疾病的50岁妇女说，她认为自己在大约15天后就康复了，但随后受到疲劳的打击。
她说：这就像我被车碾过一样。重力感觉就像是给我的四肢施加了额外的压力。
另一名34岁的妇女说，在做完剥蔬菜等短期任务后，她需要休息一下。
她说：我必须做一件家务活，坐下来15到20分钟，然后再做下一件，这让我很沮丧。这就像削土豆皮一样，我不能在剥完胡萝卜后直接削皮。
患者说，他们觉得自己的症状已经消失，他们担心永远不会完全从病毒中恢复。
一位50岁的女士说：我觉得这是因为缺乏知识。我真的不能得到任何答案。
她承认这是一种新疾病，但她说：即使只有一位医生稍微研究了一下，然后又回到我身边，也没有发生过这种情况。
另一名42岁的妇女说，她仍然担心自己在3月份感染冠状病毒后永远不会好转。
与此同时，一名英国男子被发现遭受了与新冠肺炎有关的第五起突发性听力损失报告。
这名45岁的男子患有哮喘，但在感染冠状病毒之前就已经很健康了，并在症状开始10天后入院治疗。
他在重症监护病房呆了一段时间，并接受了30天的插管，还患有高血压、贫血、呼吸机相关性肺炎和血栓等并发症。
根据BMJ Case Reports上的报告，他在离开重症监护病房一周后注意到左侧耳鸣和听力损失，没有听力问题的病史。
来自伦敦大学学院和皇家国家咽喉鼻耳医院的专家表示，突发性感音神经性听力损失(SSNHL)在重症监护环境中很容易被漏掉。
他们呼吁医务人员提高认识，并补充说，早期服用类固醇可能提供恢复听力的最好机会。
他们写道：尽管研究的数量很少，但考虑新冠肺炎与中国国家冰球协会之间存在联系的可能性是有意义的。
这是英国首例报道的新冠肺炎感染后感音神经性耳聋病例。
考虑到病毒在人群中的广泛存在和听力损失的显著发病率，进一步调查这一点是很重要的。
note_en:
People who suffer from "long COVID" are finding themselves too fatigued to do even simple tasks, according to a new study.
 Some of those with persistent and long-lasting symptoms following a COVID-19 infection are left almost bed-ridden many months after the worst of the virus.
  The study, which is published in the British Journal of General Practice, featured interviews with 24 long COVID sufferers about their symptoms and the lack of help available.
  None of those interviewed were admitted to hospital during their initial suspected or confirmed infection.
 Due to the limited community testing for the virus during the early months of the pandemic, half of the study participants had not been tested for COVID-19 - and of those who were, only one had a positive result by that time.
   A 50-year-old woman, believed to have had the disease in March, said she thought she had recovered after about 15 days but was then hit by fatigue.
  She said: "It was just like I'd been run over... gravity felt like it was applying extra on my limbs.
     Another woman, 34, said she needed a break even after short tasks such as peeling vegetables.
 She said: "I have to do a chore, sit down for 15, 20 minutes and then do the next, which frustrates me. It's like peeling potatoes, I can't peel the carrots straight afterwards."
 Sufferers said they felt their symptoms were dismissed and that they feared never fully recovering from the virus.
 One woman, 50, said: "I feel like there's a lack of knowledge. And I really wasn't able to get any answers."
 She acknowledged the illness was new, but said: "Just even for one doctor to look into it a bit and come back to me - didn't happen."
 Another woman, 42, said she still fears she is "never going to get better from this" after having coronavirus in March.
   It comes as a a British man was revealed to have suffered just the fifth reported case of sudden hearing loss linked to COVID-19.
 The man, 45, had asthma but was otherwise fit and well before he caught the coronavirus and was admitted to hospital 10 days after his symptoms began.
 He spent time in intensive care and was intubated for 30 days, also suffering from complications including high blood pressure, anaemia, ventilator-associated pneumonia and a blood clot.
 He noticed left-sided tinnitus and hearing loss a week after leaving intensive care, having had no history of hearing problems, according to the report in BMJ Case Reports.
 Experts from University College London and the Royal National Throat Nose and Ear Hospital said sudden onset sensorineural hearing loss (SSNHL) can "easily be missed in an intensive care setting".
 They called for more awareness among medics, adding that an early course of steroids could offer the best chance of recovering hearing.
 They wrote: "Despite the low numbers of studies, it is significant to consider the possibility of a relationship between COVID-19 and SSNHL.
 "This is the first reported case of sensorineural hearing loss following COVID-19 infection in the UK.
 "Given the widespread presence of the virus in the population and the significant morbidity of hearing loss, it is important to investigate this further."
title: 24岁的“长时间休息”研究：一名女性在剥完蔬菜后需要短暂休息
title_en: 'Long Covid' study of 24: one woman needs short breaks after peeling vegetables

note:
Auury与高露洁(Colgate)和喜力(Heineken)等大企业合作，维护其生产线上的机器，已经筹集了5500万美元的资金。联合创始人兼首席执行官萨尔·约斯科维茨(Saar Yoskovitz)在接受采访时表示，这笔资金将用于扩大其向客户提供的服务，以及建立其全球客户基础，以及与其合作的公司生态系统，以便它可以扩大其目标客户的类型，包括小企业和扩大规模。
D系列由Qumra Capital牵头，这是一家总部位于以色列的晚期风险投资公司(Auury成立于海法，现在纽约设有第二个总部)，Insight Venture Partners、Eclipse Ventures、慕尼黑再风险投资(Munich Re Venture Capital)、高通风险投资公司(Qualcomm Ventures)和勒尔·希佩奥风险投资公司(Lerer Hippeau Ventures)也参与其中-都是这家初创公司的前支持者。
Auury成立于2011年，但自2014年以来一直处于秘密状态，现在已经筹集了1.06亿美元，我们从一位接近该公司的消息人士那里了解到，估值在2亿美元到3亿美元之间。
该公司的扩张正值更广泛的工业技术世界的一个有趣的时刻，就竞争格局而言也是如此。
“商业内幕”(Business Insider)9月份的一份报告发现了亚马逊和监管机构的文件，这些文件指出亚马逊正在开发一项名为AWS Thor的新服务，这听起来像是它在向企业提供一项服务方面可以直接与Auury竞争，这项服务可以监控机器的各种物理属性，并检测机器可能出现故障的时间，或者至少需要维护的时间。消息人士告诉我们，雷神最快可能在本月推出-向Auury注资，将其服务扩展到更多类型的客户，并在最初诊断的基础上提供更多分析，确实非常及时。
就更广泛的工业市场而言，资金和预兆的增长正处于关键时刻。首先，这样的服务可能是物联网全球可行商业模式的首批真正支柱之一-这是一个期待已久的市场，到目前为止回报一直不佳。
除此之外，或许更直接的是当今世界的状况。具体地说，新冠肺炎和全球卫生大流行的更广泛影响带来了更多的紧迫性，并对商界起到了提振作用，商界正在加速向更多数字化系统的转变，允许更远程的工作流程。
我们听到很多关于“知识型员工”(指工作日在电脑前或办公桌前工作的人的通称)的情况，但那些在一线、在流水线上工作和维护生产线的人也是如此。
Auury不仅提供工具来检测机器何时没有以最佳能力工作，而且还将这些信息提供给维护人员，以便在故障无法修复之前更快地修复它们。然后，它还一直在建立一个更广泛的合作伙伴生态系统，以订购和交付修复这些机器所需的部件。
“商店里的货架空空如也，突然之间，每个制造商都需要工具来更好地协作和风险管理，”约斯科维茨说。他说，机器故障，特别是当他们超速工作时，可能会产生严重的供应连锁反应。
Auury的合作伙伴包括德国的ProPac、芬兰的Caverion和意大利的Pluriserservice、拉丁美洲和南美的Fuse IoT以及北美的42 North。这也是像保险巨头慕尼黑再保险这样的战略投资者也发挥作用的地方。其他原始设备制造商和服务提供商包括Grundfos、Carrier、Trane和DSV。
去哪儿资本管理合伙人Sivan Shamri Dahan在一份声明中表示：“新冠肺炎危机暴露了全球供应链的严重失灵。”“由于需求增加导致的基础产品短缺，加上制造商无法满足供应需求，显示出迫切需要对制造业世界进行数字化改造。在这场数字革命中发挥重要作用的预兆正在经历巨大的增长。其扩张和执行的记录使其成为大型IIoT市场的世界领先者。我们很高兴支持Auury，并与其一起踏上激动人心的旅程。“
note_en:
Augury, which works with large enterprises like Colgate and Heineken to maintain machines in their production and distribution lines, has raised $55 million in funding. Co-founder and CEO Saar Yoskovitz said in an interview that the funding will be used to expand the services that it provides to customers, as well as to build out its global customer base, and the ecosystem of companies that it works with so that it can expand the kinds of customers it targets to include smaller businesses and scale-ups.
 The Series D is being led by Qumra Capital, a late-stage VC firm based out of Israel (Augury was founded in Haifa and now has a second HQ in New York), with participation also from Insight Venture Partners, Eclipse Ventures, Munich Re Venture Capital, Qualcomm Ventures and Lerer Hippeau Ventures — all past backers of the startup.
  Augury, founded in 2011 but out of stealth since 2014, has now raised $106 million, and we understand from a source close to the company that the valuation is in the range of between $200 million and $300 million.
 The company’s expansion is coming at an interesting point in the wider world of industrial tech, as well as in terms of the competitive landscape.
 A report in September from Business Insider uncovered documents at Amazon and with regulators that pointed to the company working on a new service it’s called AWS Thor, which sounds like it could directly rival Augury in providing a service to businesses to monitor various physical attributes of machines and detect when they might be breaking down, or at least need maintenance. Sources tell us that Thor could launch as soon as this month — making the capital injection into Augury to expand its service to more types of customers, and to provide more analytics on top of the initial diagnostics very well timed indeed.
 In terms of the wider industrial market, the funding and Augury’s growth are coming at a key moment. For starters, services like these are, perhaps, one of the first real anchors of a viable business model around the world of IoT — a long-anticipated market that has failed to come good on returns up to now.
 Beyond that, and perhaps more immediately, is the state of the world today. Specifically, Covid-19 and the wider repercussions of the global health pandemic have brought more urgency and served as a fillip to the business world, which is accelerating the shift to more digital systems that allow for more remote working processes.
 We hear a lot about how that is playing out for “knowledge workers” — the generic term for people who spend their working days in front of computers or at desks — but the same is going for those on the front lines, working on assembly lines, and maintaining production lines.
 Augury is not only providing tools to detect when machines are not working at optimal capacity, but is then providing that information to maintenance people to fix them faster before the breakdowns are irreparable. And then, it also has been building a wider ecosystem of partners to order and deliver parts that are needed to fix those machines.
 “The shelves are empty in stores, and all of the sudden every manufacturer needs tools for better collaboration and risk management,” said Yoskovitz, who said that machine failures especially when they are working in overdrive can have severe supply knock-on effects.
 Augury’s partners include ProPac in Germany, Caverion in Finland and Pluriservice in Italy, Fuse IoT in Latin and South America, and 42 North in North America. And this is also where strategic investors like Munich Re, the insurance giant, also come into play. Other OEMs and services providers include Grundfos, Carrier, Trane, and DSV.
 “The Covid-19 crisis has revealed critical failures in the global supply chain,” said Sivan Shamri Dahan, managing Partner at Qumra Capital, in a statement. “The shortage in basic products due to the increased demand, coupled with the inability of manufacturers to meet supply requirements, demonstrated an urgent need to digitally transform the manufacturing world. Augury, which plays a significant role in this digital revolution, is experiencing tremendous growth. Its track record of expansion and execution, positions it to be a world leader in the large IIoT market. We are happy to support Augury and join it on its exciting journey.”
title: Auury斥资5500万美元开发根据振动、声音和温度预测机器故障的技术
title_en: Augury taps $55M for tech that predicts machine faults from vibration, sound and temperature

note:
该公司最初和酒店业的其他企业一样，受到了地区性应对新冠肺炎疫情的沉重打击，现在已经恢复过来，在其管理的墨西哥城周围的200家酒店里，已经达到了总产能的近90%。
该公司由首席执行官尼科·巴拉维德(Nico Barawid)和首席运营官玛丽亚·德尔·卡门·赫雷亚斯·萨拉扎(María del Carmen Herrerías Salazar)共同创立。巴拉维德曾担任Nova Credit国际扩张主管和波士顿咨询顾问，后者曾在墨西哥最大的酒店运营商之一Grupo Psidente工作。
两年前，两人在墨西哥城的一次烧烤上相遇，并开始谈论如何更新酒店业，充分利用Airbnb的个体短期租赁模式，并将其与客人对连锁酒店的质量控制和标准相结合。
“我想从消费者的角度定义一种产品，”巴拉维德说。“我想让它存在。”
在SARS-CoV-2爆发之前，Casai的房间主要是通过HotelTonight或Expedia等旅游合作伙伴预订的。现在，由于首席技术官安德烈斯·马丁内斯(Andres Martinez)的工作，该公司拥有了直接活跃的直接预订业务。
该公司的新融资由Andreessen Horowitz牵头，包括公司的文化领导基金、Kaszek Ventures、Monashees Capital、Global Founders Capital、Liquid 2 Ventures以及包括Nova Credit、Loft、Kavak和Runa创始人在内的个人投资者的额外承诺。
Casai还设法从TriplePoint Capital获得了高达2500万美元的债务安排，使其总现金收益达到4800万美元的股权和债务。
这轮大融资在一定程度上要归功于该公司令人信服的价值主张，它不仅为客人提供了配备专有智能硬件中心和Casai应用程序的住宿场所，还为客人提供了谷歌主页、智能灯和Chromecast电视，还有一个休息室，客人可以在入住前或退房后入住。
虽然该公司现在的愿景集中在拉丁美洲，但其管理团队肯定看到了创建全球品牌和业务的机会。
创始团队还包括曾在麦肯锡工作的首席营收官阿尔贝托·拉莫斯(Alberto Ramos)和曾在旅游和生活方式公司Selina工作的首席增长官丹尼尔·赫尔曼(Daniel Hermann)。设计负责人Alexa Backal曾在盖亚设计公司工作，其体验副总裁克里斯蒂娜·克雷斯波(Cristina Creso)曾负责WeWork的国际设计工作室。
安德森·霍洛维茨律师事务所(Andreessen Horowitz)普通合伙人、Casai董事会成员安吉拉·斯特兰奇(Angela Strange)说，“为了成功抓住这个机会，一个团队需要把消费技术、设计、酒店、房地产和金融服务的专业知识结合起来，开发出提供一流体验所需的世界级业务。”当我见到Nico和Maricarmen时，很明显，他们在操作上专注于激光，在垂直领域拥有独特的融合专业知识，对消费者体验有着独特的看法。“
note_en:
The company, which initially was as hit hard by regional responses to the COVID-19 pandemic as other businesses in the hospitality industry has recovered to reach nearly 90 percent of total capacity on the 200 units it manages around Mexico City.
 The company was co-founded by chief executive  Nico Barawid, a former head of international expansion at Nova Credit and consultant with BCG, and chief operating officer María del Carmen Herrerías Salazar, who previously worked at one of Mexico’s largest hotel operators, Grupo Presidente.
  The two met two years ago at a barbecue in Mexico City and began speaking about ways to update the hospitality industry taking the best of Airbnb’s short term rental model of individual units and pairing it with the quality control and standards that guests expect from a hotel chain.
 “I wanted to define a product from a consumer angle,” said Barawid. “I wanted this to exist.”
 Before the SARS-Cov-2 outbreak Casai’s units were primarily booked through travel partners like HotelTonight or Expedia. Now the company has a direct brisk direct booking business thanks to the work of its chief technology officer, a former engineer at Google named Andres Martinez.
 The company’s new financing was led by Andreessen Horowitz and included additional commitments from the firm’s Cultural Leadership Fund, Kaszek Ventures, Monashees Capital, Global Founders Capital, Liquid 2 Ventures, and individual investors including the founders of Nova Credit, Loft, Kavak and Runa.
 Casai also managed to nab a debt facility of up to $25 million from TriplePoint Capital, bringing its total cash haul to $48 million in equity and debt.
  The big round is in part thanks to the company’s compelling value proposition, which offers guest not only places to stay equipped with a proprietary smart hardware hub and the Casai app, but also a Google Home, smart lights, and Chromecast-kitted televisions, but also a lounge where guests can stay ahead of their check-in or after check-out.
 And while the company’s vision is focused on Latin America now, its management team definitely sees the opportunity to create a global brand and business.
 The founding team also includes a chief revenue officer, Alberto Ramos, who worked at McKinsey and a chief growth officer, Daniel Hermann, who previously worked at the travel and lifestyle company, Selina. The head of design, Alexa Backal, used to work at GAIA Design, and its vice president of experience, Cristina Crespo, formerly ran WeWork’s international design studio.
 “To successfully execute on this opportunity, a team needs to bring together expertise from consumer technology, design, hospitality, real estate and financial services to develop world-class operations needed to deliver on a first-class experience,” said Angela Strange, a general partner at Andreessen Horowitz, who’s taking a seat on the Casai board. “It was obvious when I met Nico and Maricarmen that they are operationally laser-focused and have uniquely blended expertise across verticals, with unique views on the consumer experience.”
title: 合并Airbnb和传统酒店模式，墨西哥城Casai筹集2300万美元在拉丁美洲增长
title_en: Merging Airbnb and the traditional hotel model, Mexico City’s Casai raises $23 million to grow in Latin America
